front: Concept: atoms spontaneously deviate from their path, creating randomness and explaining free will in Epicurean and Lucretian physics
Philosopher: used the example of a square tower appearing round to argue that all sense perception is true, but judgment of perception can be fallible
Philosopher: conjectured that the gods occupy areas devoid of atoms called metakosmia and have no concern for mortals
Concept: daily Epicurean recitation from the 'principal doctrines,' a fourfold remedy arguing against fear of gods and death
Concept: Epicurean inscription on gravestones translating to 'I was not, I was, I am not, I do not care'
back: Non fui, fui, non sum, non curo

front: Philosopher: led a circle of Epicureans in Naples whose writings were found in the Villa of the Papyri at Herculaneum
Philosopher: taught at a school called 'the Garden,' whose gate reportedly read 'Stranger, here you will do well to tarry'
Philosopher: established three criteria of truth: feelings, preconceptions (prolepseis), and sense-perceptions
Philosopher: credited with introducing the problem of evil, popularized as a trilemma in David Hume's Dialogues Concerning Natural Religion
